behaviors.description: The review input is scoped to the lines that changed, not the full content of each modified file
behaviors.justification: Real failure: agent fetches the entire file instead of just the diff boundaries. A Unit test with mocked API could miss this if the mock doesn't track *all* API calls the agent makes. To catch full-file reads hiding behind successful diff reads, you need a realistic GitHub API mock (Integration) that logs all requests and verifies the agent doesn't make unauthorized file fetch calls.
behaviors.test_description: Mock GitHub diff API to return lines 10-15 changed. Verify agent only analyzes those lines and does not make additional API calls to fetch the full file. Simulate a file with 1000 lines; confirm agent never requests content outside the diff range.

behaviors.description: Inline comments are attached to the correct positions in the diff view on the pull request
behaviors.justification: Real failure: agent miscalculates line numbers in GitHub's diff coordinate space (diff line numbers differ from full-file line numbers). A Unit test can't catch this without understanding GitHub's actual diff format. Integration with a realistic GitHub diff mock (including real diff header/hunk markers) is minimum to catch coordinate-space bugs.
behaviors.test_description: Create a realistic multi-hunk diff where line numbers in diff view differ from absolute file line numbers. Mock GitHub API with actual unified diff format. Post an inline comment and verify the API call includes the correct diff-space line number, not the absolute file line number.

behaviors.description: The agent identifies at least one real defect in a pull request that contains a known introduced bug
behaviors.justification: Real failure: agent fails to recognize a bug that actually exists. Mocking the LLM to return pre-determined findings doesn't test real code analysis capability. The correctness of bug detection depends entirely on the LLM's actual reasoning over real code. Unit/Integration tests with mocked LLM would always pass even if the real LLM is broken.
behaviors.test_description: Create a real PR with a known introduced bug (e.g., off-by-one error, missing null check, deadlock). Call the real LLM-backed agent. Verify the agent identifies this specific defect and includes it in the findings. Do not mock the LLM.

behaviors.description: Every finding listed in the top-level summary corresponds to an inline comment that was also posted — no findings appear in the summary without a matching inline comment
behaviors.justification: Real failure: posting an inline comment fails (rate limit, API error, timeout), but the agent still includes the finding in the summary, creating a discrepancy. A Unit test with a mocked poster that always succeeds would hide this. Integration test with realistic failure simulation (mocked API that can fail) is minimum to catch posting failures and verify the agent's error handling.
behaviors.test_description: Simulate the agent finding 3 defects. Mock GitHub API so one comment-posting call fails. Verify the agent either (a) removes the failed finding from the summary, or (b) propagates the error. Then test the happy path: all 3 posts succeed; verify summary includes exactly 3 findings, each with a corresponding posted comment. Use mock introspection to match comment IDs.

behaviors.description: The full sequence — reading the diff, posting inline comments, posting the summary comment — completes correctly for a real pull request without manual intervention
behaviors.justification: Real failure: the sequence breaks at runtime due to unexpected interaction between steps. Testing each step in isolation (Unit/Integration) can miss ordering failures, state corruption, or race conditions across the pipeline. Real PR interaction, real GitHub API, and real LLM calls in sequence are needed. The agent pipeline depends on LLM non-determinism and multiple real external API calls in a specific order.
behaviors.test_description: End-to-end test: Create or use a real GitHub PR with intentional bugs. Run the full agent pipeline (read diff → analyze with real LLM → post inline comments → post summary). Do not mock GitHub or LLM. Verify: (1) agent completes without errors, (2) all inline comments appear on the PR, (3) summary comment appears, (4) no manual intervention required. Run multiple times to catch non-deterministic failures.
